title: T-004 — scanner capability detection lib/scanner/detect.ts (06-a)
prompt:
# T-004 — scanner capability detection

PRD: docs/prd/06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §6 D5, §8.1 FR-SC-FE-011, §11.1, §11.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Provide `detectScannerCapabilities()` returning `{ hardware, camera, manual: true, deviceType, preferredMode }` so every scanner page can choose the right input affordance.

## Implementation contract
1. Module `apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/detect.ts`:
   - UserAgent regex: `/Zebra|Datalogic/i`, `/Honeywell/i`, `/iPhone|iPad/i`, `/Android/i`.
   - Hardware heuristic: input event timing analysis — wedge bursts < 50ms per char, terminator Enter — exposed as `classifyKeystrokeBurst(events: KeyboardEvent[])`.
   - Camera detection: `navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia` capability probe, lazy (no permission prompt during detection).
   - `preferredMode` rule: hardware > camera > manual.
2. Type `ScannerCapabilities` exported.
3. Vitest with mocked navigator (use jsdom + custom userAgent fixtures); cover all 7 device buckets in §11.1.

## Files
- apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/detect.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/index.ts [modify]
- apps/scanner/__tests__/detect.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given navigator.userAgent matches /Zebra/, when detectScannerCapabilities runs, then deviceType='zebra' and preferredMode='hardware'
2. Given navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia is undefined, when detectScannerCapabilities runs, then camera=false and preferredMode never returns 'camera'
3. Given a 6-char keystroke burst all <50ms apart ending in Enter, when classifyKeystrokeBurst runs, then it returns 'wedge'

## Test strategy
- RED: write apps/scanner/__tests__/detect.test.ts covering all 7 §11.1 device buckets + camera-absent + camera-present before implementation
- Run 'pnpm --filter scanner test detect'

## Risk red lines
- Do not call getUserMedia at module load (no permission prompts during detection)
- Do not throw if navigator is undefined (SSR safety — return manual-only)
- Do not hardcode device list outside the regex constants

pipeline_inputs.description: Detect scanner hardware/camera/manual capabilities and preferred mode for SSR-safe scanner UI bootstrap.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Create apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/detect.ts exporting detectScannerCapabilities + classifyKeystrokeBurst + ScannerCapabilities type. Implement UserAgent regexes per §11.1, lazy mediaDevices probe (no permission prompt), keystroke timing analysis. SSR-safe (must not throw when navigator is undefined). Vitest fixtures cover all 7 §11.1 buckets + camera-absent + a 6-char wedge burst.

Wave0 scanner parity note: use `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/scanner/login.jsx:1-220` as prototype reference and include mobile viewport 390x844 screenshot + Playwright trace artifacts in closeout.
out_of_scope: Do not implement the camera scanner component here (T-014)
Do not implement feedback audio/haptic here (T-005)
Do not write the scanner layout here (T-012)
Do not implement P2 flows here: transfer-order (TO) creation, replenishment, cycle-count, inquiry full traceability — P1 stubs only
Do not implement offline queue write-path here (P2); P1 keeps offline-safe scan buffer + indicator only
acceptance_criteria: Given the production scanner UI/wiring renders, when compared to `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/scanner/flow-receive.jsx:94-239` at mobile viewport 390x844, then structural, visual, and interaction parity is preserved (touch targets >=48dp, scanner dark tokens, badge+PIN auth, scanner.* permissions enforced).
Given navigator.userAgent matches /Zebra/, when detectScannerCapabilities runs, then deviceType='zebra' and preferredMode='hardware'
Given navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia is undefined, when detectScannerCapabilities runs, then camera=false and preferredMode is never 'camera'

Given a 6-char keystroke burst all <50ms apart ending in Enter, when classifyKeystrokeBurst runs, then it returns 'wedge' UI closeout includes 390x844 screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: write detect.test.ts covering all 7 §11.1 device buckets + camera-absent + wedge burst classifier before implementation
For UI parity, capture 390x844 screenshots and Playwright trace/video artifacts; attach artifact paths in closeout per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`.
risk_red_lines: Do not call getUserMedia at import time
Do not crash on SSR (no navigator)
Do not depend on @zxing in this module
Do not bypass scanner.* permission namespace when wiring server actions
Do not regress operator-friendly touch targets (>=48dp) or mobile viewport 390x844 evidence
cross_module_dependencies: 02-settings: PIN policy, locale, scanner device defaults
